title: Prevent Tariff Abuse Act
stage_label: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and in addition to the Committee on Ways and Means, for a period to be sub
plain_summary: The Prevent Tariff Abuse Act stops the President from using emergency powers to put extra taxes on goods coming into the United States from other countries. Right now, when the President declares a national emergency, they can use special powers to control trade and put limits on imports. This bill would take away the President's ability to add these extra costs and limits on foreign goods during emergencies. The goal is to prevent the President from misusing emergency powers to control trade with other countries.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and in addition to the Committee on Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such prov
subjects: Presidents and presidential powers, Vice Presidents

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Prevent Tariff Abuse Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Prohibition on the Imposition of Import Duties and Quotas from Presidential Authorities Under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the International Emergency Economic Powers Act. The change adds new language that limits what the President can do during emergencies. The new rule says that when the President uses emergency economic powers, those powers do not include the ability to put taxes called duties on goods coming into the United States. The President also cannot use emergency powers to set limits called quotas on how much of certain products can be brought into the country. This includes both regular quotas that completely limit amounts and tariff-rate quotas which are limits that trigger higher taxes after a certain amount is imported.
